routing.guidance: Classifies the problem as one where key variables are visible but future values remain uncertain.

boundary.guidance: Usually keeps scope relatively tight because the main variables are already visible.

structure.guidance: Supports higher confidence in the structural map and pushes attention to sensitivities and thresholds.

scenarios.guidance: Prefers tighter branches and narrower probability bands.

question_generation.guidance: Favors range, threshold, and sensitivity questions.

evidence_planning.guidance: Encourages direct measurement of visible variables and disciplined range updates.

decision_logic.guidance: Supports action logic tied to explicit thresholds while warning against false precision.

signal_translation.guidance: Signals should narrow ranges and confirm threshold movement.

monitoring.guidance: Monitoring should be threshold-based and relatively regular.

render.guidance: Should show confidence ranges without pretending certainty.
